mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month expansions in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand strength that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series declines, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and output that generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during late-cycle expansions, with a negative or zero Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price index readings increase inflation expectations and prompt tighter monetary policy forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases show no negative predictive power for 5-day forward returns (Rank IC > -0.01) across all market conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% quarter-over-quarter confirms robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are not significantly positive when GDPC1 growth surpasses the 2.5% threshold, with a hit rate below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent payroll growth above 150,000 and declining unemployment rates signal a strengthening labor market that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative 20-day returns in over 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production indices indicate contracting manufacturing activity that triggers immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days during monetary tightening cycles.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during tightening cycles, with a positive Rank IC exceeding 0.02 in out-of-sample tests.
